creator: exploringlondon
caption:
ad CHEAP DAYS OUT IN THE UK - PART 2 - YORK! This was honestly one of the cutest cities we’ve been to, there are so many adorable, independent shops, food markets and so much history here, so here’s your itinerary!
 
Book a train direct with @lner up to 2 weeks in advance for the cheapest rate and use your railcard for 30% off! The Shambles is a must when in York, with cute shops like Monk Bar Chocolatiers (get the chocolate shot in the chocolate cup!), The Shop that Must Not be Named (lots of Harry Potter themes here!) as well as York Castle Museum and Clifford’s Tower if you want a beautiful view! Ending with a famous cream tea for under £15 per person and dinner at a Nepalese gem where you can get a full plate of momo for under £11!
 
Book your ticket to York now, SAVE this itinerary for your trip and follow for part 3 - which city should we visit next?!

transcription.text: Welcome back to our series Cheap Days Out in the UK where each week we'll be going to a new location on a budget trying to save as much money as we can. And for part two we're in York. To get there we booked a train from London Kings Cross direct with LNER two weeks in advance to get a cheaper rate and you can save up to 30% using a rail card. And once you're at York station walk towards the Shambles. You can actually walk along the historic city wall for some incredible views along the way and the Shambles is the cutest street. Apparently an inspiration for Harry Potter's Diagon Alley. You'll also find the cutest chocolate shop here called Monk Bar Chocolatiers where you can get this chocolate shot which is so worth it in an edible chocolate cup. They also have a ghost merchants here because apparently York is one of the most haunted cities in Europe. So you can take away your own ghost then head to the Shambles market to grab a bite to eat and then head to York Castle Museum home to the oldest recreated Victorian streets. It really feels like you've gone back in time here. And right outside the museum is Clifford's Tower which you can climb for incredible views. Make sure you go for cream tea at Betty's Cafe Tea Rooms and grab some dinner before you train home. Then to the Nepalese restaurant Tass. They do a plate of incredible momo for under £11. Save this video for your next cheap day out in the UK and follow for part three.
